title: Tame a blocking call inside async code
description: Locate blocking work running on a compute pool and move it onto a bounded blocking executor.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the symptom: throughput collapses, latency spikes, or the app stalls under load. 2. Find blocking calls (JDBC, synchronous IO, Thread.sleep, Await) on the compute/global EC. 3. Move them to a dedicated blocking ExecutionContext, or wrap in blocking { } (Future) / IO.blocking (Cats-Effect). 4. Confirm the compute pool is no longer occupied by blocking work. 5. Load-test to verify recovery. Rollback point: before changing executor wiring. Validation: under load the compute pool stays responsive and a test confirms blocking work runs on the blocking pool, not the compute pool.
